mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with an accuracy exceeding 55%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day SPX returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive power vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.6% signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.6%, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings forecasts to drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero 60-day forward return in > 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% annualized confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month during normal economic expansions.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2% fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with an accuracy above 55%, or the signal reverses direction during periods of rising interest rates.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in the index of industrial production reflects immediate improvements in factory output and capacity utilization, transmitting directly to equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns within the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows no predictive power for 5-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.
